headline: Fintech Lending Serves 2.8 Million Unbanked Malaysians
context: Licensed P2P lending platforms disbursed RM18.5 billion in 2025 serving 2.8 million Malaysians lacking traditional bank access.

cards.big: P2P lending disbursed RM18.5 billion to 2.8 million unbanked in 2025
cards.sub: Fintech fills credit gap left by traditional banks lending requirements

cards.big: Average P2P loan RM6,600 with 12% interest versus bank minimum 18%
cards.sub: Default rate across licensed platforms averaged 4.2% within limits

cards.big: SC licensed 11 platforms but 28 unlicensed operators also active
cards.sub: Unlicensed platforms charge 35-80% interest targeting desperate borrowers

cards.big: B40 households account for 65% of borrowers with RM2,800 average income
cards.sub: Debt servicing consumes 42% of income for typical B40 P2P borrower

cards.big: Fintech lending tests whether financial inclusion comes with adequate borrower protection
2.8 million unbanked served by fintech. Alternative lending fills gaps banks created through exclusion.
